summary: Completed testing of tutoring center management system. Backend APIs 100% working (16/16 tests passed). Frontend admin panel working correctly. Critical feature: teacher_rate is now saved when lessons are created, and teacher balance uses this rate for calculations.

ui_bugs.issue: Student level (sınıf) not displayed in edit form. Database has values like '9. Sınıf', '5. Sınıf' but Select component expects '5', '6', '7', '8'. Shows 'Sınıf seçin' placeholder instead of actual value.

passed_tests: Teacher edit form loads name and phone correctly
Teacher 'Branş ve Fiyatlandırma' dialog opens correctly
Student edit form loads all fields (name, parent, phone, payment_freq, notes)
Ödeme Günü (payment_freq) field displayed and working
All reference data endpoints working (branches, lesson-types, seasons, bank-accounts)
action_items: Fix student level value mismatch: Database stores '9. Sınıf', '5. Sınıf' format but Select expects '5', '6', '7', '8'. Either normalize database values or update Select to handle both formats.
critical_code_review_comments: AdminStudentForm.jsx line 193: Select value={formData.level} won't match when DB has '9. Sınıf' but SelectItem value='5'. Need value normalization.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend teacher_rate feature is working correctly. Main issue is frontend level select value mismatch. Database has mixed formats: '9. Sınıf', '5. Sınıf', '6. snıf', '7. sınıf'. Select component expects simple values: '5', '6', '7', '8'.
rca_of_the_issue: Student level select not showing value in edit form because: 1) Database stores level as full text like '9. Sınıf', '5. Sınıf' 2) Select component has values '5', '6', '7', '8' 3) When formData.level='9. Sınıf' and SelectItem value='5', no match occurs 4) Fix: Either normalize DB values to simple numbers OR add value mapping in frontend
